req.title: Patient portal login (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three testable scenarios: (1) successful SSO login establishing a session and emitting an AuditEvent — positive path via EP; (2) repeated failed login attempts triggering HTTP 429 rate-limiting with patient notification — negative path via NEG/DT; (3) the exact boundary attempt count (N-1 vs N) that transitions from allowed to rate-limited — boundary path via BVA/STC. Each test is self-contained using fixtures that reset rate-limit state and SSO stubs, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_successful_sso_login_establishes_session_and_emits_audit_event
preconditions: Rate-limit counter is reset for the test patient
AuditEvent sink is observable (API or log)
actions: POST /portal/login with a valid SSO assertion payload
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query AuditEvent endpoint for the patient's login event
expected: HTTP 200 with session token in response body
AuditEvent record exists with action=login, outcome=success, patient reference correct
trace: R-HLT-098: session established on valid SSO
R-HLT-098: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_successful_sso_login_establishes_session_and_emits_audit_event(
    api_client,           # <fixture>: pre-configured httpx/requests session
    valid_sso_assertion,  # <fixture>: returns signed SSO payload dict
    reset_rate_limit,     # <fixture>: resets rate-limit for test patient
    audit_event_query,    # <helper_fn>: queries FHIR AuditEvent API
    test_patient_id,      # <fixture>: stable patient ID for this test run
):
    payload = valid_sso_assertion(patient_id=test_patient_id)

    response = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "session_token" in body, "No session_token in response"
    assert len(body["session_token"]) > 0

    events = audit_event_query(
        patient_id=test_patient_id,
        action="login",
        outcome="success",
    )
    assert len(events) >= 1, "AuditEvent not emitted for successful login"
    evt = events[-1]
    assert evt["action"] == "login"
    assert evt["outcome"] == "success"
    assert test_patient_id in evt["patient_ref"]

tests.name: test_repeated_failed_login_returns_429_and_notifies_patient
preconditions: Rate-limit counter is reset for the test patient
An invalid SSO assertion is constructable
Notification sink is observable (email stub or API)
actions: POST /portal/login with invalid SSO assertion N times (exceeding rate-limit threshold)
Check notification stub for patient alert
Query AuditEvent for each failed attempt
expected: Attempt exceeding threshold returns 429 Too Many Requests
429 response body contains rate-limit error message
Patient notification fired (email/push) referencing failed login
AuditEvent emitted for every attempt including the rate-limited one
trace: R-HLT-098: failed attempts shall be rate-limited
R-HLT-098: notify the patient on rate-limit
R-HLT-098: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD = 5  # <config_const>: matches fhir-lite setting

def test_repeated_failed_login_returns_429_and_notifies_patient(
    api_client,
    invalid_sso_assertion,  # <fixture>: returns unsigned/bad SSO payload
    reset_rate_limit,
    audit_event_query,
    notification_stub,      # <fixture>: captures outbound notifications
    test_patient_id,
):
    payload = invalid_sso_assertion(patient_id=test_patient_id)

    for attempt in range(1, RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD + 1):
        resp = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
        assert resp.status_code == 401, (
            f"Attempt {attempt}: expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

    over_limit = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429, (
        f"Expected 429 on attempt {RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD + 1}, "
        f"got {over_limit.status_code}"
    )
    assert "rate" in over_limit.text.lower(), "No rate-limit message in 429 body"

    notices = notification_stub.get_notifications(patient_id=test_patient_id)
    assert any("login" in n["message"].lower() for n in notices), (
        "Patient was not notified of excessive failed attempts"
    )

    events = audit_event_query(patient_id=test_patient_id, action="login")
    assert len(events) >= RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD + 1, (
        "AuditEvent count less than number of attempts"
    )

tests.name: test_login_attempt_at_rate_limit_boundary_transitions_to_blocked
preconditions: Rate-limit counter is pre-seeded to threshold minus one (N-1)
An invalid SSO assertion is constructable
actions: Pre-seed rate-limit counter to N-1 via test-support API
POST /portal/login with invalid assertion (attempt N — last allowed)
POST /portal/login with invalid assertion (attempt N+1 — first blocked)
Verify AuditEvent emitted for both attempts
expected: Attempt exactly at threshold (N) returns 401, not 429
State transition from ALLOWED to BLOCKED occurs precisely at N+1
AuditEvent records exist for both boundary attempts
trace: R-HLT-098: rate-limit boundary — BVA on attempt count
R-HLT-098: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD = 5  # <config_const>

def test_login_attempt_at_rate_limit_boundary_transitions_to_blocked(
    api_client,
    invalid_sso_assertion,
    reset_rate_limit,
    seed_rate_limit_counter,  # <helper_fn>: sets counter via admin/test API
    audit_event_query,
    test_patient_id,
):
    seed_rate_limit_counter(
        patient_id=test_patient_id,
        count=RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD - 1,
    )

    payload = invalid_sso_assertion(patient_id=test_patient_id)

    # Attempt N: boundary — must still return 401, not 429
    resp_at_limit = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
    assert resp_at_limit.status_code == 401, (
        f"Attempt N={RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD}: expected 401 (not yet blocked), "
        f"got {resp_at_limit.status_code}"
    )

    # Attempt N+1: one beyond boundary — must return 429
    resp_over_limit = api_client.post("/portal/login", json=payload)
    assert resp_over_limit.status_code == 429, (
        f"Attempt N+1={RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD + 1}: expected 429, "
        f"got {resp_over_limit.status_code}"
    )

    events = audit_event_query(patient_id=test_patient_id, action="login")
    assert len(events) >= 2, "Expected AuditEvents for both boundary attempts"
